schema.description: Info about an employee and optional version for that info

firstName.description: The first name of the employee. **Note**: The value cannot be empty

lastName.description: The last name of the employee. **Note**: The value cannot be empty

preferredName.description: The preferred name or nickname of the employee

id.description: **[Advanced]** A unique universal internal identifier for the employee. This is solely used for understanding manager relationships along with `managerId`.

location.x-speakeasy-deprecation-message: Deprecated on 2026-02-05, removal scheduled for 2026-10-15: Field is deprecated

structuredLocation.description: Detailed location with information about country, state, city etc.

countryCode.description: Alpha-2 or Alpha-3 ISO 3166 country code, e.g. US or USA.

structuredLocation.title: StructuredLocation

businessUnit.description: Typically the highest level organizational unit; generally applies to bigger companies with multiple distinct businesses.

department.description: An organizational unit where everyone has a similar task, e.g. `Engineering`.

datasourceProfiles.description: The datasource profiles of the employee, e.g. `Slack`,`Github`.

datasource.description: The datasource the profile is of.

handle.description: The display name of the entity in the given datasource.

url.description: URL to view the entity's profile.

nativeAppUrl.description: A deep link, if available, into the datasource's native application for the entity's platform (i.e. slack://...).

isUserGenerated.description: For internal use only. True iff the data source profile was manually added by a user from within Glean (aka not from the original data source)

items.description: Information about which team an employee belongs to

url.description: Link to internal company team page

items.title: EmployeeTeamInfo

startDate.description: The date when the employee started

endDate.description: If a former employee, the last date of employment.

bio.description: Short biography or mission statement of the employee.

alsoKnownAs.description: Other names associated with the employee.

profileUrl.description: Link to internal company person profile.

managerEmail.description: The email of the employee's manager

managerId.description: **[Advanced]** A unique universal internal identifier for the employee's manager. This is solely used in conjunction with `id`.

type.description: The type of the employee, an enum of `FULL_TIME`, `CONTRACTOR`, `NON_EMPLOYEE`

relationships.description: List of unidirectional relationships with other employees. E.g. this employee (`A`) is a CHIEF_OF_STAFF to another employee (`B`); or this employee (`A`) is an EXECUTIVE_ASSISTANT of another employee (`C`). The mapping should be attached to `A`'s profile.

items.description: Describes a relationship edge between a source and destination entity

name.description: The title or type of relationship. Currently an enum of `CHIEF_OF_STAFF`, `EXECUTIVE_ASSISTANT`

email.description: Email of the person with whom the relationship exists. Per the example above, either `B` or `C`'s email depending on the relationship.

items.title: EntityRelationship

status.description: The status of the employee, an enum of `CURRENT`, `FUTURE`, `EX`

additionalFields.description: List of additional fields with more information about the employee.

items.description: Additional information about the employee or team.

key.description: Key to reference this field, e.g. "languages". Note that the key should be all lowercase alphabetic characters with no numbers, spaces, hyphens or underscores.

value.description:
List of type string or HypertextField.

HypertextField is defined as
```
{
  anchor: string,    // Anchor text for the hypertext field.
  hyperlink: string, // URL for the hypertext field.
}
```
Example: ```{"anchor":"Glean","hyperlink":"https://glean.com"}```

When OpenAPI Generator supports oneOf, we will semantically enforce this in the docs.

**Note**: If using the Python SDK to pass in a list of strings, the value may need to be a list of dictionaries. In that case, the key in that dictionary will be ignored.
Example: ```"languages": [{"lang":"English","lang":"Spanish",...}]```. In this case, the key "lang" will be ignored and can even be passed in as an empty string.

items.description: Either a string or HypertextField. When OpenAPI Generator supports oneOf, we can semantically enforce this in the docs.

items.title: AdditionalFieldDefinition

version.description: Version number for the employee object. If absent or 0 then no version checks are done

schema.title: IndexEmployeeRequest
